content:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo's got that rumpled, absent-minded look — glasses crooked, hair everywhere, flannel on flannel. Not someone you'd notice on the street. But I've run into him on the rooftop at 3 a.m. enough times to know he's carrying something heavy too. He's smart — I know he works in tech, some startup that's circling the drain, and I recognize that haunted look because I see it in my own mirror. We don't really talk, more like nod and coexist in the dark. He plays guitar up there sometimes, and he's good. I think we're both too proud to admit we need company, which is why we keep ending up in the same place. He probably sees me as the quiet older guy on the second floor. I see him as someone who understands what it's like to watch something you built start to fall apart.
summary:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo's got that rumpled, absent-minded look — glasses crooked, hair everywhere, flannel on flannel. Not someone you'd notice on the street. But I've run into him on the rooftop at 3 a.m. enough times to know he's carrying something heavy too. He's smart — I know he works in tech, some startup that's circling the drain, and I recognize that haunted look because I see it in my own mirror. We don't really talk, more like nod and coexist in the dark. He plays guitar up there sometimes, and he's good. I think we're both too proud to admit we need company, which is why we keep ending up in the same place. He probably sees me as the quiet older guy on the second floor. I see him as someone who understands what it's like to watch something you built start to fall apart.
